title: npm package compromise through Actions cache and OIDC

advisories.title: Malware in npm packages exfiltrates cloud credentials, GitHub tokens, and SSH keys

workflow_patterns.name: pull_request_target_runs_fork_code
workflow_patterns.snippet:
on:
  pull_request_target:
    paths: ['packages/**', 'benchmarks/**']

jobs:
  benchmark-pr:
    permissions:
      contents: read
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6.0.2
        with:
          ref: refs/pull/${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}/merge
      - run: pnpm nx run @benchmarks/bundle-size:build

workflow_patterns.name: release_workflow_has_oidc_write
workflow_patterns.snippet:
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

permissions:
  contents: read
  id-token: write

jobs:
  release:
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6.0.2
      - run: pnpm install
      - run: pnpm test

known_gaps: GHSA malicious-package advisories are not currently classified as blocking malware.
No GitHub Actions scanner exists for pull_request_target, cache trust boundaries, OIDC token scope, or mutable action refs.
No npm package tarball analysis exists for malicious optionalDependencies or install-time payload fingerprints.
pnpm-lock.yaml and yarn.lock inventory are not currently implemented.
